title: Please participate in brief research interview to help shape rural healthcare funding for OpenEMR users
question: OpenEMR users, we need your help to shape the direction of rural interoperability funding in the USA. For the next five years the federal government is allocating 1 billion dollars each year in funding to the states to help with rural health solutions and transformation. James Shalaby on behalf of Elimu Informatics is looking for volunteers willing to participate in a brief interview to learn about your current workflows, pain points, and the resources that would most effectively support your team in delivering chronic care. They are looking for a wide variety of perspectives from clinical staff, program administrators, technology administrators, etc. The feedback for this research is going directly to your state leaderships to help them allocate federal dollars in programs that will support you and your fellow OpenEMR users. If you’re available, you can select a time that works best for you here: NIH I-Corp Research on Quality | FreeBusy or simply reply to this email at icorps@elimu.io Here is a more in depth interview info sheet from Elimu Informatics: We are writing to you as part of an NIH-supported program in which Elimu Informatics is participating. The objective of this initiative is to better understand the operational challenges and needs of community health centers and primary care practices in managing patients with chronic illnesses. Additionally, insights gathered from these interviews will help inform strategies on how states may allocate dollars from the new Rural Health Transformation Program (RHTP) to best assist organizations like yours in addressing your unique needs. We are conducting brief interviews to learn about your current workflows, pain points, and the resources that would most effectively support your team in delivering chronic care. Please be assured that this interview is strictly for research and strategic planning purposes; it is not a sales call. Given your leadership role in [Quality / Population Health / Care Delivery / IT], your perspective would be invaluable to this initiative. Would you be available for a brief, 30-minute interview? You can select a time that works best for you here: NIH I-Corp Research on Quality | FreeBusy or simply reply to this email at icorps@elimu.io .
